sgTip: Highbridge is one of the last easy grind spots before the game accelerates. If Yuna hasn't learned Reflect yet, stay and farm the Save Sphere until she does — it's the single most valuable spell in the Seymour Natus fight.

subLocations.name: Highbridge
subLocations.prose: After escaping the Via Purifico, the full party reunites on Highbridge for a final reckoning with Seymour. Two Save Spheres flank the bridge — one at each end — and this is an excellent place to grind AP before triggering the boss fight. The most important prep target: if Yuna hasn't learned Reflect yet, stay and farm until she does. Reflect is central to the Seymour Natus strategy, both for protecting your party and for turning the Mortibody's Cura spells back onto you. Beyond Reflect, get every character into Overdrive mode before you engage — front-loading burst damage is essential because Seymour's defenses escalate sharply as his HP falls. After the fight, all of Yuna's aeons are fully restored, including Bahamut if you completed the Bevelle Cloister.

bosses.strategy: Tidus, Yuna, and Auron each have Trigger Commands to boost their Strength or Magic Defense — not worth the turn. Open with Overdrives if available; only summon aeons that can immediately use their own Overdrive, as Seymour Banishes summoned aeons after a single attack. Have Lulu tag Seymour with Bio for sustained damage throughout the fight. Use Reflect on Seymour to redirect the Mortibody's Cura spells back onto your team — this turns the enemy's healing into your healing. Seymour's defenses strengthen as HP drops: Break spells after losing 1/3, Flare spells after 2/3, and eventually he casts Protect on himself. Frontload your heavy attacks to maximise damage before the Protect lands. If you're struggling, target the Mortibody instead — when its HP drains, it siphons HP from Seymour, and if you can outpace its Cura casts it will drain Seymour to death.
